description: Remove the cluster-prompt path from `task-mgr curate dedup`. Pair-judgment becomes the only mode. Replace item-chunking with pair-batching so every cosine-≥-threshold candidate pair lands in at least one LLM batch and gets recorded in `dedup_dismissals`. Hard-require embeddings (no legacy fallback) and abstract `OllamaEmbedder` behind a new `Embedder` trait so a second provider can drop in later. Remove the `--pair-mode` flag.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use that can't be reasonably extended (the Embedder trait IS justified because it's named scaffolding for additional providers)
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong or what config to inspect
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Cross-batch candidate pairs that silently end up in no LLM batch (the leak this PR exists to fix)
`unwrap()` in production paths — use `map_err`/`?` with `TaskMgrError` variants

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All tests pass with scoped `cargo test` (full suite at REVIEW-001)
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required by this PR's scope (which DOES require breaking changes to dedup CLI and embedder construction)

userStories.title: Embedder trait + factory; OllamaEmbedder behind the trait
userStories.description:
Introduce `pub trait Embedder: Send + Sync` in `src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs` with methods `provider() -> &'static str`, `model() -> &str`, `endpoint() -> &str`, `is_available() -> Result<bool, String>`, and `embed_batch(&[&str]) -> TaskMgrResult<Vec<Vec<f32>>>`. Provide a default method `embed(&str) -> TaskMgrResult<Vec<f32>>` that delegates to `embed_batch`. Implement the trait for the existing `OllamaEmbedder` (signatures already match — adapt only as needed).

Add a factory `pub fn make_embedder(provider: &str, endpoint: &str, model: &str) -> TaskMgrResult<Box<dyn Embedder>>` that returns the Ollama impl for `"ollama"` and a `TaskMgrError::ConfigError` for any other string (no other providers ship in this PR — the factory exists so a follow-up can add one without touching dedup).

Add `embedding_provider: Option<String>` to `ProjectConfig` at `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs` (alongside the existing `ollama_url` and `embedding_model` fields). Default value when unset is `"ollama"`. Wire the new field through `read_project_config`.

Migrate the following call sites from constructing `OllamaEmbedder` directly to going through `make_embedder`:
- `try_embed_learning` (`src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:~352`)
- `try_embed_learnings_batch` (`src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:~402`)
- `curate_embed` Ollama wiring (`src/commands/curate/mod.rs:~1175–1199`)

DO move callers to `make_embedder`.
DO keep `VectorBackend` (`src/learnings/retrieval/vector.rs`) on a concrete `OllamaEmbedder` field — it consumes `base_url()` directly when building `TaskMgrError::OllamaUnreachable`, and rationalizing that error variant is out of scope.
DO NOT introduce a second provider in this PR — the trait is scaffolding.
DO NOT modify `OllamaEmbedder`'s internal HTTP logic (timeouts, agents) — only add the trait impl.

Edge cases:
- `make_embedder` called with unknown provider string: return `ConfigError` naming the bad value.
- `embedding_provider` field missing from config: defaults to `"ollama"` (no error).
- `embed_batch` called with empty slice: returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (matches existing OllamaEmbedder behavior).

Known-bad to guard against:
- Implementing `embed` directly on the trait instead of as a default method that calls `embed_batch` — would force every impl to duplicate the single-element extraction logic.
- Forgetting to add `: Send + Sync` to the trait — would prevent the worker thread queue from accepting `Box<dyn Embedder>` once dedup picks it up in FEAT-003.
acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `pub trait Embedder: Send + Sync` exists in `src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs` with the five methods named in the description and a default `embed` method
Positive: `impl Embedder for OllamaEmbedder` covers all five required methods; `provider()` returns the string `"ollama"`
Positive: `pub fn make_embedder(provider, endpoint, model) -> TaskMgrResult<Box<dyn Embedder>>` returns Ollama impl for `"ollama"`
Positive: `try_embed_learning`, `try_embed_learnings_batch`, and `curate_embed` all construct their embedder via `make_embedder` instead of `OllamaEmbedder::new` directly
Positive: `ProjectConfig` has `pub embedding_provider: Option<String>` field with serde rename matching neighbors (e.g., camelCase `embeddingProvider`)
Negative: `VectorBackend` still holds a concrete `OllamaEmbedder` field — this PR does NOT migrate retrieval to the trait
Negative: No new concrete `Embedder` impl other than `OllamaEmbedder` is added in this commit
Negative: `make_embedder` does NOT silently fall back to Ollama for unknown providers — it returns `ConfigError`
Unit test: `make_embedder("ollama", url, model)` returns `Ok(Box<dyn Embedder>)` whose `provider()` returns `"ollama"`
Unit test: `make_embedder("openai", _, _)` returns `Err(ConfigError)` whose message contains the string `"openai"`
Unit test: trait-object dispatch — a fn taking `&dyn Embedder` correctly calls each of the four required methods on an OllamaEmbedder instance (use a mocked HTTP layer or skip HTTP-bound methods)
Unit test: `ProjectConfig` default for `embedding_provider` resolves to `"ollama"` when the field is absent from JSON

Known-bad: an impl that defines `embed_batch` to delegate to `embed` (instead of the other way around) would still compile but cause infinite recursion — verify the default `embed` calls `embed_batch`, not the reverse
CONTRACT: every existing caller of `OllamaEmbedder::new` enumerated in the description either (a) moves to `make_embedder` or (b) is the documented exception (`VectorBackend`)
Failure mode: if `make_embedder` is called before `ProjectConfig` is read: caller is responsible for passing explicit `provider`/`endpoint`/`model` args (no global state)
userStories.notes: OllamaEmbedder's existing surface (is_available, embed, embed_batch, model, base_url) maps almost 1:1 — rename `base_url` → `endpoint` only in the trait, keep `base_url()` as a `pub fn` on the concrete struct for VectorBackend's continued use. Reuse: `OllamaEmbedder::new` at src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs:39. ProjectConfig fields at src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:~11,34–39. Learning [1401]: ProjectConfig pattern with Option<String> + sensible defaults is the established shape. Learning [1944]: keep OllamaEmbedder's 3s/30s timeout discipline — don't break the consistency reranker shares. Learning [1918]: mockito mocks Ollama HTTP endpoints — useful for the trait dispatch test.
qualityDimensions: Trait object dispatch through `Box<dyn Embedder>` works in single-threaded contexts (Send + Sync sufficient for the worker queue)
`make_embedder` factory pattern matches the rest of the codebase (e.g., `read_project_config` style)
OllamaEmbedder's existing HTTP timeout discipline (3s/30s) preserved
Error messages from `make_embedder` name the bad provider string verbatim so operators can grep config
edgeCases: Unknown provider string: `ConfigError` with the bad value in the message
Empty endpoint/model strings: pass through (let the concrete impl decide whether to reject)
`embed_batch` with empty `texts` slice: returns `Ok(Vec::new())` without HTTP call

failureModes.expectedBehavior: `TaskMgrError::ConfigError` with the offending provider string named in the message

failureModes.cause: Ollama unreachable at the configured endpoint
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Same behavior as today — `is_available()` returns `Err(String)` describing the connection failure

userStories.title: cluster_by_embedding_similarity returns candidate pairs; rename pair prompt; delete cluster prompt
userStories.description:
Refactor `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` (`src/commands/curate/dedup.rs:73`) to return a struct carrying both the existing clusters AND the actual cosine-≥-threshold pairs:

```rust
pub struct EmbeddingClusterResult {
    pub clusters: Vec<Vec<i64>>,
    /// Sorted unique (lo, hi) pairs with cosine(lo, hi) >= threshold.
    pub candidate_pairs: Vec<(i64, i64)>,
}
```

The existing pairwise loop already computes each cosine; record qualifying `(lo, hi)` pairs as the loop runs. Output `candidate_pairs` sorted ascending by `(lo, hi)`, deduplicated. Clusters retain their current shape (each inner Vec sorted ascending; outer Vec sorted by `c[0]`).

Then delete `build_dedup_prompt` (the cluster-shaped prompt, `src/commands/curate/dedup.rs:~126–184`) entirely. Rename `build_pair_judgment_prompt` (`src/commands/curate/dedup.rs:~210–266`) to `build_dedup_prompt` (canonical name; signature: `pub fn build_dedup_prompt(items: &[DeduplicateLearningItem], candidate_pairs: &[(i64, i64)], similarity_threshold: f64) -> String`). Update the `pub use` re-export in `src/commands/curate/mod.rs:12` to drop `build_pair_judgment_prompt`.

This task does NOT yet change the orchestrator. After this commit, the orchestrator continues to call the renamed `build_dedup_prompt` from item-based sub-batches (the pair-mode sketch's current behavior under a different name). Coverage leak still exists — FEAT-003 fixes it.

DO update all test call sites to the new `build_dedup_prompt` signature with three args.
DO update orchestrator call sites in `src/commands/curate/mod.rs` to the new prompt-builder name.
DO delete tests that exercised the removed cluster prompt OR the renamed pair prompt under the old name.
DO NOT yet remove `--pair-mode` flag or change `DedupBatchInput` shape — those are FEAT-003.
DO NOT yet remove `dismissed_pairs_within` / `prune_batch_to_judgable` / `finalize_dedup_batch` / `build_standard_batches` / `unjudged_pairs_within` / `is_fully_dismissed` — those die in FEAT-003 when their callers go away.
DO NOT change `parse_dedup_response` or `validate_clusters` — response shape unchanged.

Edge cases:
- Empty embeddings input: `EmbeddingClusterResult { clusters: vec![], candidate_pairs: vec![] }`.
- N=2 with cosine below threshold: no clusters, no pairs.
- Transitive case (cosine(A,B) ≥ T, cosine(B,C) ≥ T, cosine(A,C) < T): clusters contains `[A, B, C]` (union-find); candidate_pairs contains `[(A,B), (B,C)]` only — no `(A,C)`.
- Duplicate pair surfacing (shouldn't happen with i<j iteration, but defensive): sort + dedup before returning.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Including transitive (A,C) in candidate_pairs because the union-find groups them — silently makes pair-mode behave like the old cluster mode, defeating the user-chosen sparsity invariant.
- Forgetting to update the rustdoc on `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` to mention the new return type — future readers will mis-interpret.
- Test-call-site updates that pass `&[]` to satisfy the new signature instead of constructing the pair list the test actually means to send.
acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `EmbeddingClusterResult` struct is `pub` and has the documented fields with the documented orderings
Positive: `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` returns `EmbeddingClusterResult`; clusters field matches the prior return value semantics
Positive: `candidate_pairs` contains exactly the `(lo, hi)` pairs where `cosine(lo, hi) >= threshold`, sorted ascending, deduplicated
Positive: `build_dedup_prompt` (after rename) takes three args and is reachable via `pub use dedup::build_dedup_prompt`
Negative: The cluster-shaped `build_dedup_prompt` (with `already_judged_distinct` parameter) is REMOVED — no longer compiles, no longer exported
Negative: `build_pair_judgment_prompt` no longer exists — only the canonical `build_dedup_prompt` remains
Negative: Transitive pairs (A,C) where `cosine(A,C) < threshold` MUST NOT appear in `candidate_pairs` even when A and C share a union-find root via some B
Unit test: `test_cluster_by_embedding_similarity_returns_threshold_pairs` — 3-vector input with A·B and B·C above threshold but A·C below: `candidate_pairs == [(A, B), (B, C)]` exactly
Unit test: existing `test_cluster_*` tests still pass (clusters semantics unchanged) — adapt to the new return type

Unit test: `test_dedup_prompt_lists_candidates_and_restricts_body` (renamed from `test_pair_judgment_prompt_*`) — `build_dedup_prompt(items, candidate_pairs, threshold)` lists each pair as `(lo, hi)`, restricts items in the prompt body to those referenced by some candidate pair, keeps the UNTRUSTED warning and boundary delimiter
Unit test: `build_dedup_prompt` with `candidate_pairs.is_empty()` produces a prompt that elicits `[]` (still valid; LLM will just see no pairs to judge)

Known-bad: a refactor that emits pairs from `groups.into_values()` (union-find roots) instead of from the pairwise cosine loop would include transitive (A,C); the targeted test pins the correct behavior
CONTRACT: every test in `src/commands/curate/tests.rs` that calls `build_dedup_prompt` uses the three-arg signature; grep for two-arg calls returns zero hits in `src/`
Failure mode: if `candidate_pairs` has duplicates (shouldn't, but defensively): sort + dedup before returning, do NOT panic
userStories.notes: Existing pairwise loop at src/commands/curate/dedup.rs:86–93 already computes each cosine — record qualifying pairs there. UnionFind helper at lines 20–55 is unchanged. The renamed `build_dedup_prompt` body is identical to today's `build_pair_judgment_prompt` (it already restricts items to referenced learnings and keeps the UNTRUSTED guard). Test call sites that pass `&[]` for already_judged_distinct become `&[]` for candidate_pairs — same shape, different meaning. Learning [1414]: embedding pre-filter pattern is the architecture being refined here. Learning [1819]: cover empty/singleton/two-item/transitive edge cases in tests.
qualityDimensions: Single pairwise loop computes both cluster membership AND candidate_pairs — no second O(N²) pass
candidate_pairs is the actual cosine-≥-threshold edge set, NOT the union-find cluster's C(N,2) projection
Rustdoc on `EmbeddingClusterResult` explicitly states the (lo < hi) canonical ordering and the no-transitive-pair invariant
Tests pin both the cluster semantics AND the new candidate_pairs semantics independently
edgeCases: Empty embeddings: EmbeddingClusterResult with both fields empty
Single learning: both fields empty (no pairs to compute, no multi-member cluster)
Two learnings below threshold: clusters empty, candidate_pairs empty
Two learnings at or above threshold: clusters has one cluster of 2, candidate_pairs has the one (lo, hi)
Transitive A~B~C without A·C: clusters has [A,B,C], candidate_pairs has [(A,B), (B,C)] only

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return empty result, no panic, no allocation work
Single cluster of all N items, C(N,2) candidate pairs

userStories.title: Pair-batching with full coverage; hard-require embeddings; remove --pair-mode flag
userStories.description:
The heart of the PR. Replace item-chunking with pair-batching in `curate_dedup`, hard-require embeddings (no legacy fallback), and remove the now-redundant `--pair-mode` flag.

ADD: a new helper in `src/commands/curate/dedup.rs`:
```rust
pub(crate) fn pack_pair_batches(
    items: &[DeduplicateLearningItem],
    candidate_pairs: &[(i64, i64)],
    max_items: usize,
) -> Vec<(Vec<DeduplicateLearningItem>, Vec<(i64, i64)>)>
```

Algorithm — greedy first-fit over the candidate pair list pre-sorted ascending by `(lo, hi)`. For each pair `(a, b)`:
1. Iterate existing batches IN INSERTION ORDER.
2. Accept the first batch whose item set ∪ `{a, b}` has ≤ `max_items` items. Append the pair (and any newly-introduced items) there.
3. If no batch fits, start a new batch seeded with `{a, b}` and the pair.

Empty `candidate_pairs` → empty output. Deterministic given identical input.

REWRITE the embedding-cluster branch in `curate_dedup` (`src/commands/curate/mod.rs:~813–907`) to:
1. Call `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` and destructure the new `EmbeddingClusterResult`.
2. Filter `candidate_pairs` to keep only those NOT in the loaded `dismissals` HashSet.
3. Project the filtered pairs onto their owning cluster (build a `HashMap<i64, usize>` from id to cluster index, then bucket pairs accordingly).
4. For each cluster with at least one un-dismissed pair: hydrate the items via `item_map`, call `pack_pair_batches(&cluster_items, &cluster_pairs, max_items)`, and push each resulting `(items, pairs)` as a `DedupBatchInput`.

COLLAPSE `DedupBatchInput` to:
```rust
struct DedupBatchInput {
    items: Vec<DeduplicateLearningItem>,
    candidate_pairs: Vec<(i64, i64)>,
}
```
(Drop `already_judged_distinct` field and the `Option<>` wrapper on `candidate_pairs`.)

SIMPLIFY the worker prompt-construction (`src/commands/curate/mod.rs:~620–630`) to always call `build_dedup_prompt(&items, &candidate_pairs, threshold)`.

DELETE the skip filter wrap (the `filter_map` block at `~920–944`) entirely — the batch builder now only emits batches with non-empty candidate pairs, so `is_fully_dismissed` cannot fire. Replace with a direct `Vec<DedupBatchInput>` assignment.

DELETE these helpers (no remaining callers): `finalize_dedup_batch`, `prune_batch_to_judgable`, `dismissed_pairs_within`, `build_standard_batches`, `unjudged_pairs_within`, `is_fully_dismissed`. Delete their tests as well.

HARD-REQUIRE embeddings: when `emb_list.is_empty()` OR `params.embed_model.is_empty()`, return `TaskMgrError::ConfigError("curate dedup requires an embedding provider with stored embeddings. Run `task-mgr curate embed` first, or check `.task-mgr/config.json` `embeddingProvider`.".into())`. Delete the `if emb_list.is_empty()` branch and the outer `else` (embed_model empty) branch.

REMOVE the `--pair-mode` CLI flag:
- `src/cli/commands.rs:~1694–1695` — delete the clap arg and the `pair_mode: bool` field on the destructure.
- `src/main.rs:~1408,1426` — drop `pair_mode` from the `CurateAction::Dedup` destructure and from the `DedupParams { ... }` literal.
- `src/commands/curate/types.rs:~244,263` — drop `pub pair_mode` field from `DedupParams` and `pair_mode: false` from `Default`.

UPDATE `DedupResult.clusters_skipped` semantics: keep the field (it has `serde(default = 0)` for JSON-output compat) but always set to zero. Drop it from the operator stderr summary. Note in rustdoc that it's reserved for future use.

UPDATE the operator stderr summary (`src/commands/curate/mod.rs:~962–991`) to a single line:
```
Processing {total_batches} batch(es) (concurrency={N}), {pair_count} candidate pair(s) across {cluster_count} embedding cluster(s)
```
Fires whenever total_batches > 0. When total_batches == 0 (zero candidate pairs after dismissal filter), emit "No candidate pairs to examine (every cosine-≥-threshold pair already in dedup_dismissals)."

UPDATE `compute_dismissal_pairs` rustdoc ONLY (do not change body) to note that under the new flow `batch_ids` is the union of items participating in the batch's candidate pairs, which may include cosine-<-threshold pairs (the LLM was never asked about them). These ARE dismissed by transitivity of the embedding pre-filter — semantically defensible and operationally useful.

DO populate `candidate_pairs` for every batch (no `Option`, no skip filter — the batch builder ensures non-empty).
DO route the dedup orchestrator's embedding loader through `make_embedder` (from FEAT-001) so the hard-error message names the configured provider.
DO use `dismissals.contains(&p)` for the filter — `dismissals` is already `HashSet<(i64, i64)>` from `load_dismissals`.
DO NOT keep `--pair-mode` as a no-op flag or as a deprecation shim — hard-remove.
DO NOT keep any of the deleted helpers as `#[allow(dead_code)]` — fully remove.
DO NOT modify `parse_dedup_response`, `validate_clusters`, `merge_cluster`, `compute_dismissal_pairs`, or `record_dismissals` bodies — they are unchanged.
DO NOT change `VectorBackend` — it stays concrete-Ollama.

Edge cases:
- Cluster with all pairs already dismissed: zero pairs after filter → no batch emitted → no LLM call (replaces the old `clusters_skipped` count).
- Cluster N=2, single pair, un-dismissed: one batch with both items and the one pair.
- Cluster N=9, all 36 pairs un-dismissed, max_items=7: pack_pair_batches produces multiple batches; UNION of all batches' pairs == the 36 input pairs.
- Pair list `[(1,2),(1,3),...,(1,K)]` (star), max_items=7: packs into `ceil((K-1)/6)` batches sharing the anchor 1.
- Pair count > C(max_items, 2): forces multiple batches.
- No embeddings stored: hard error before any work.
- Empty `--pair-mode` invocation: clap rejects with "unexpected argument".

Known-bad to guard against:
- A first-fit that iterates batches in REVERSE insertion order — still correct, but non-deterministic output across refactors; pin insertion-order in the algorithm AND test.
- A skip filter that re-checks `is_fully_dismissed` after pruning — would short-circuit batches the new batch builder correctly skipped before they got created. Don't reintroduce.
- A test for the algorithm that asserts batch count (brittle, depends on packing) instead of the union-coverage invariant — the right test asserts `union(batches[i].pairs) == input_pairs`.
- Keeping `unjudged_pairs_within` because "it's still useful" — the new orchestrator uses `dismissals.contains(p)` directly; no caller remains.
acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `pack_pair_batches(items, candidate_pairs, max_items)` returns a Vec where the union of all batches' `candidate_pairs` equals the input `candidate_pairs` exactly (set equality)
Positive: every emitted batch has `items.len() <= max_items`
Positive: identical calls produce identical output (deterministic)
Positive: `DedupBatchInput` has exactly two fields: `items` and `candidate_pairs: Vec<(i64, i64)>` (no Option, no `already_judged_distinct`)
Positive: worker always calls `build_dedup_prompt(&items, &candidate_pairs, threshold)` — no match on prompt-mode
Positive: orchestrator branch construct `DedupBatchInput` from `pack_pair_batches` output; no `chunks(max_cluster_batch)` call remains
Positive: `curate_dedup` with `emb_list.is_empty()` returns `TaskMgrError::ConfigError` whose message mentions `"task-mgr curate embed"` and `"embeddingProvider"`

Positive: `compute_dismissal_pairs` rustdoc has a new paragraph explaining the post-refactor semantics (records cosine-<-threshold pairs that share a batch with a candidate pair, by transitivity of embedding pre-filter)
Negative: `--pair-mode` flag is GONE — `task-mgr curate dedup --pair-mode` exits non-zero with clap's "unexpected argument" message
Negative: `pair_mode: bool` field on `DedupParams` is GONE — `cargo check` errors anywhere it's still referenced

Negative: `finalize_dedup_batch`, `prune_batch_to_judgable`, `dismissed_pairs_within`, `build_standard_batches`, `unjudged_pairs_within`, `is_fully_dismissed` all DELETED — no `#[allow(dead_code)]` retention
Negative: no `batch.chunks(max_cluster_batch)` call remains in `curate_dedup` — grep returns zero hits in `src/commands/curate/mod.rs`
Negative: no cross-batch pair leaks — every cosine-≥-threshold pair the embeddings surface lands in some batch's `candidate_pairs`
Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_covers_every_input_pair` — N=9 items, 12 sparse candidate pairs, max=7: assert union of all batches' candidate_pairs equals the input set (no missing, no extras)
Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_single_cluster_fits_in_one_batch` — N ≤ max_items: exactly one batch containing all items and all input pairs

Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_forces_split_when_pair_count_exceeds_clique` — 4-item cluster with all 6 pairs candidates, max_items=3: produces ≥ 2 batches, each with items.len() ≤ 3, union covers all 6 pairs
Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_respects_max_items` — every emitted batch has items.len() ≤ max_items across multiple random input sizes
Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_is_deterministic` — same input twice produces bitwise-identical output Vec

Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_star_pattern_packs_efficiently` — input `[(1,2),(1,3),(1,4),(1,5),(1,6),(1,7),(1,8)]` (anchor=1, 7 pairs), max_items=7: first batch = `{1,2,3,4,5,6,7}` + first 6 pairs (`(1,2)..(1,7)`); second batch = `{1,8}` + last pair `(1,8)`; total 2 batches
Unit test: `test_pack_pair_batches_empty_pairs` — empty candidate_pairs returns empty Vec, regardless of items
Integration test: `test_dedup_rejects_when_no_embeddings_configured` — `embed_model = ""` returns ConfigError with the documented message

Integration test: `test_curate_dedup_records_every_candidate_pair_end_to_end` — 9-item cluster, all 36 pairs are candidates, mock LLM returns `[]`, `batch_size=7`. After run: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dedup_dismissals` for these IDs equals at least 36 (may be slightly higher due to cosine-<-threshold incidental pairs from `compute_dismissal_pairs` semantics — but never lower)

Integration test: `test_curate_dedup_zero_llm_calls_on_clean_rerun` — populate `dedup_dismissals` with every cosine-≥-threshold pair, then run `curate_dedup`. Mock `spawn_claude` to panic if called. Assert successful return and stderr summary names zero batches

Known-bad: a `pack_pair_batches` impl that uses `items.into_iter().chunks(max_items)` (item-chunking instead of pair-packing) would pass the determinism + max-items tests but FAIL the coverage invariant when pair count exceeds C(max_items, 2)
CONTRACT: `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` is called exactly once per `curate_dedup` invocation (no second O(N²) pass)
CONTRACT: every consumer of the old `DedupBatchInput.already_judged_distinct` field has been removed; grep returns zero hits in `src/`
Failure mode: empty embeddings table (post-config-validation): ConfigError before any LLM call

Failure mode: a single cluster produces a giant pair list (e.g., 200 items, ~20k pairs): `pack_pair_batches` still terminates and produces deterministic output; consider emitting an `eprintln!` warning at >16 batches per cluster
userStories.notes:
Hot zone for the refactor:
- batch builder at src/commands/curate/mod.rs:813–907
- DedupBatchInput at ~565–574
- worker prompt at ~620–630
- skip-filter wrap at ~920–944
- operator output at ~962–991
- compute_dismissal_pairs rustdoc at ~1587–1607 (body UNCHANGED)
- helpers to delete: finalize_dedup_batch (~1495), prune_batch_to_judgable (~1508), dismissed_pairs_within (~1439), build_standard_batches (~1453), unjudged_pairs_within (~1466), is_fully_dismissed (~1427)
- CLI flag at src/cli/commands.rs:1694
- main.rs Dedup arm at 1408,1426
- DedupParams at src/commands/curate/types.rs:244,263

Learning [1837]: cluster_internal_pairs MUST be excluded from dismissals — already handled by compute_dismissal_pairs unchanged. Learning [1833]: this PR completes the skip-aware framing. Learning [1818]: load/record/clear CRUD set stays; only is_fully_dismissed is removed (the new flow doesn't need a fully-dismissed check). Learning [2690]: normalize_pair canonical form (lo < hi) MUST be preserved; pack_pair_batches outputs pairs in canonical form. Learning [1822]: NO new migration in this PR — v18 dedup_dismissals is sufficient. Learning [1420]: WIRE-FIX-tagged review check — verify the new orchestrator branch is reachable from `task-mgr curate dedup`.
qualityDimensions: Coverage invariant: union of all emitted batches' candidate_pairs equals the post-dismissal-filter input (no missing pairs, no spurious extras)
Determinism: identical input across two calls produces bitwise-identical batch list
Item-bound: no batch's items.len() exceeds max_items
Single pass over candidate_pairs in pack_pair_batches (no nested loops over the full pair list)
Orchestrator no longer reads `dismissed_pairs_within` — uses `dismissals.contains(p)` directly
Hard-error path produces a single clear stderr line naming `task-mgr curate embed` and the config key
Zero-pair clusters generate zero batches AND zero stderr noise (`No candidate pairs to examine` line)
edgeCases: Empty candidate_pairs after dismissal filter: zero batches emitted, no LLM call
Cluster N=2, one pair, un-dismissed: one batch with the one pair
N=9 cluster with all 36 pairs candidates, max=7: multiple batches; union covers all 36
Star pattern `[(1,2),(1,3),...,(1,K)]`: packs into `ceil((K-1)/(max_items-1))` batches with anchor reused
Pair count > C(max_items, 2): forces multiple batches
Empty embeddings table: hard error before LLM
Operator passes `--pair-mode`: clap rejects with unexpected-argument error

failureModes.expectedBehavior: TaskMgrError::ConfigError with message naming `task-mgr curate embed` and the config key

failureModes.cause: Single embedding cluster of 200 items (~20k pairs)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Algorithm terminates with deterministic output; emit eprintln warning if a single cluster generates > 16 batches suggesting `--batch-size` increase

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md Dedup Dismissal Memory + Embedding sections
userStories.description:
Update `CLAUDE.md` (project root) to reflect the new dedup architecture. Two sections need updating: "Embedding / Ollama Configuration" (lines ~291–315) and "Dedup Dismissal Memory" (lines ~374–418).

**Embedding section changes:**
- Add a subsection titled "Embedder trait" explaining `pub trait Embedder` + `make_embedder` factory + `embedding_provider` config field. State: the dedup orchestrator and `curate embed` route through the factory; `VectorBackend` stays Ollama-concrete (rationale: `TaskMgrError::OllamaUnreachable` error variant is Ollama-named; rationalization is out of scope).
- Change the "Graceful Degradation" entry for `curate dedup` from `"works without Ollama — falls back to standard batch sizing when no embeddings exist"` to `"hard-requires an embedding provider; emits a ConfigError naming `task-mgr curate embed` when no embeddings are stored"`.

**Dedup Dismissal Memory section changes:**
- Drop the "Subsequent runs skip batches whose every C(N,2) pair is already dismissed" framing.
- Replace with: "The batch builder filters candidate pairs against `dedup_dismissals` before packing — a cluster with all candidate pairs dismissed produces zero batches and zero LLM calls."
- Drop mentions of `is_fully_dismissed` (deleted).
- Update the "When dismissals are recorded" bullet to reflect that `batch_ids` is now the union of items participating in the batch's candidate pairs (may include cosine-<-threshold pairs that share a batch with a candidate pair). Note this is semantically defensible: cosine-<-threshold pairs are distinct by the embedding pre-filter, so recording them as dismissed is correct and operationally useful (re-runs skip them).
- Note that `--pair-mode` has been removed; pair-judgment is the only path.
- Add a new subsection "Candidate pairs are cosine-≥-threshold edges, not transitive cluster pairs" explaining: `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` returns `EmbeddingClusterResult { clusters, candidate_pairs }`; transitive A~B~C without A·C above threshold does NOT make (A,C) a candidate; the LLM is only asked about pairs the embedding pre-filter actually flagged.
- Keep existing notes on: v18 schema, normalize_pair, narrow conflict suppression, multi-row INSERT, merge-map rewrite, when NOT recorded (dry_run/LLM error), --reset-dismissals, DedupResult.clusters_skipped (note now reserved for future use; always zero post-refactor), no-foreign-key invariant. All still accurate.

DO preserve the technical depth of the existing section — these notes are load-bearing for future maintainers.
DO update the rustdoc on `compute_dismissal_pairs` (already done in FEAT-003) to match the CLAUDE.md narrative.
DO NOT delete the existing notes that are still accurate.
DO NOT introduce new technical claims that aren't backed by code in this PR.

Edge cases:
- A reader who only reads CLAUDE.md should be able to understand why a re-run with merges from the prior run still triggers some LLM calls (new merged-into IDs surface fresh neighbor pairs).
- A reader who only reads CLAUDE.md should not be misled into thinking `is_fully_dismissed` is still callable.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A wholesale rewrite that loses load-bearing details (e.g., the `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` vs `INSERT OR IGNORE` distinction, the v18 CHECK constraint defense-in-depth).
- Adding new claims about behavior that aren't actually implemented (e.g., claiming the algorithm uses set-cover when it uses greedy first-fit).
acceptanceCriteria: Positive: CLAUDE.md "Embedding / Ollama Configuration" section has a new "Embedder trait" subsection describing the trait + factory + `embedding_provider` config field
Positive: CLAUDE.md "Graceful Degradation" subsection notes that `curate dedup` now hard-requires embeddings
Positive: CLAUDE.md "Dedup Dismissal Memory" section reflects pair-batching (not batch-level skip)
Positive: CLAUDE.md notes that `--pair-mode` has been removed and pair-judgment is the only path
Positive: CLAUDE.md has a new subsection explaining `candidate_pairs` = cosine-≥-threshold edges only (no transitive pairs)

Negative: CLAUDE.md does NOT mention `is_fully_dismissed`, `finalize_dedup_batch`, `prune_batch_to_judgable`, `build_standard_batches`, `dismissed_pairs_within`, or `unjudged_pairs_within` (all deleted)
Negative: CLAUDE.md does NOT mention `--pair-mode` as an active flag (only as a historical removal note, if at all)
Negative: CLAUDE.md does NOT introduce new technical claims unbacked by this PR's code
CONTRACT: every behavior described in the updated CLAUDE.md sections is grep-verifiable in `src/commands/curate/`, `src/learnings/embeddings/`, or `src/loop_engine/project_config.rs`
Failure mode: if FEAT-003's compute_dismissal_pairs rustdoc disagrees with CLAUDE.md's narrative on dismissal semantics: surface the disagreement in REVIEW-001 and update one of them
userStories.notes: CLAUDE.md sections at lines ~291–315 (Embedding) and ~374–418 (Dedup Dismissal Memory). Learning [1426]: CLAUDE.md updated alongside embedding config additions is the established pattern. Keep the section's bulleted technical-depth structure; this PR's job is to update the narrative, not restructure the doc.
qualityDimensions: Every claim is grep-verifiable in the current codebase
Load-bearing technical details (v18 CHECK, ON CONFLICT semantics, normalize_pair) preserved
Reader can understand both the new architecture AND the rationale for the design choices (sparse cosine pairs, hard-require embeddings, trait scaffolding)
No mention of removed symbols as if they still exist
edgeCases: Reader who hasn't seen this PR: should be able to understand pair-batching without reading the PR itself
Reader debugging a re-run that still calls the LLM after merges: should find the explanation (new merged-into IDs)

failureModes.cause: CLAUDE.md narrative diverges from code reality after future PRs
failureModes.expectedBehavior: REVIEW-001 and future /compound runs catch the drift; not this task's job to prevent

userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities
userStories.description:
Review all implementation in FEAT-001..FEAT-004 for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability.

DO: Check for duplicated logic between the new `pack_pair_batches` algorithm and any prior batching code; verify the Embedder trait surface is minimal (no methods that exist only for one caller); check `pack_pair_batches` and `cluster_by_embedding_similarity` aren't doing redundant O(N²) work; confirm helper deletions left no dead imports or `#[allow(dead_code)]` in their wake; check that the rustdoc on the new types (`EmbeddingClusterResult`, `Embedder`, `DedupBatchInput` post-refactor) is clear about invariants.
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before the final review.
acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication: `pack_pair_batches` algorithm logic appears in exactly one place; trait `embed` default and concrete `OllamaEmbedder::embed` aren't duplicated
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones — `curate_dedup`'s body may legitimately exceed this; if so, extract the embedding-cluster branch into a private helper)
Clear separation of concerns: pair-packing in `dedup.rs`, dismissal recording in `mod.rs`, prompt building in `dedup.rs`
Code follows existing project patterns (factory functions return `TaskMgrResult<Box<dyn Trait>>`; ProjectConfig fields use `Option<String>` with `unwrap_or_else`)
No `#[allow(dead_code)]` or commented-out blocks left behind from deletions
Imports clean: `cargo check` produces zero unused-import warnings
Rustdoc on `EmbeddingClusterResult` and `pack_pair_batches` documents the coverage invariant and ordering guarantees explicitly
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97) — atomic DB+JSON sync. If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with a one-line progress note.
qualityDimensions: DRY across modules (especially between the old and new batching paths — old code MUST be gone, not coexisting)
Single-responsibility functions
Pattern consistency with existing code
No orphan `#[allow(dead_code)]` after helper deletions

userStories.title: Code review + final verification
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation needs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Check every new function for error handling; check callers still work (especially after `Embedder` trait migration); verify no dead code; check the operator-visible stderr lines are correct on a smoke run; verify the coverage invariant via the integration test; run full `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` (no scope filter); verify `task-mgr curate dedup --pair-mode` exits non-zero with clap's unexpected-argument error; verify `task-mgr curate dedup` on a DB with zero embeddings returns the documented `ConfigError`.
DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — unconnected code is the #1 failure mode. DO NOT scope the test suite — this is THE place the full suite runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Positive: All new code reachable from production entry points (`task-mgr curate dedup` invocation; the `Embedder` trait reaches via `make_embedder`)
Positive: All errors propagated with context (map_err, not unwrap)
Positive: FULL `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean (no scope filter)
Positive: Smoke run on a real DB with seeded embeddings: `task-mgr curate dedup` runs to completion; stderr shows the new single-line summary

Positive: Smoke re-run immediately after: stderr says "No candidate pairs to examine" (assuming the prior run produced no merges; if merges occurred, document the residual `(N, neighbor)` candidates as expected)
Positive: `task-mgr curate dedup --pair-mode` exits non-zero with clap's "unexpected argument" message
Positive: `task-mgr curate dedup` on a DB with zero embeddings returns the documented `ConfigError` mentioning `task-mgr curate embed` and `embeddingProvider`
Negative: No unwrap() in production code paths
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code
Negative: No `synergyWith` / `batchWith` / `conflictsWith` populated in any spawned fix task's JSON
Documentation: CLAUDE.md updates from FEAT-004 are accurate against the final code; if any discrepancy, fix CLAUDE.md (FEAT-004 was speculative; this is the verification gate)
Documentation: `compute_dismissal_pairs` rustdoc accurately describes the new semantics (batch_ids = union of items in candidate pairs)
Task update: Remaining tasks reviewed and updated if implementation changed APIs/assumptions
Pre-existing test failures fixed (or spawned as FIX-xxx with verifyCommand if >~12 unrelated)
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks spawned via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`. If no issues: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note. Review remaining tasks — if implementation changed APIs, update task descriptions via the loop-engine sync path. Learning [1420]: known WIRE-FIX-style review pattern — grep for the new helper names from entry-point modules to verify reachability.
qualityDimensions: No unwrap in production
All new code wired to production entry point (grep-verified)
Full suite green including pre-existing
CLAUDE.md and rustdoc agree on dismissal semantics
Operator-visible stderr lines match the documented format
